phrasalVerbs.definition: To find the answer to a problem by thinking carefully.
examples: I can't figure out how this software works.
She figured out the solution in just five minutes.
We need to figure out what went wrong.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To find the answer to something or develop a plan; also to exercise.
examples: Can you work out how much we owe?
Everything worked out well in the end.
We need to work out a plan before the meeting.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To solve a problem or deal with a situation.
examples: We need to sort out this misunderstanding immediately.
Can you sort out the IT issue by 3 PM?
She sorted out the problem with the supplier.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To draw attention to something; to indicate or mention.
examples: She pointed out an error in my report.
Can you point out which bag is yours?
I'd like to point out that the deadline is tomorrow.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To produce or suggest an idea, plan, or solution.
examples: Who came up with this brilliant idea?
We need to come up with a plan quickly.
She came up with a creative solution to the problem.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To tolerate or accept something unpleasant without complaining.
examples: I can't put up with this noise any longer.
She has put up with a lot of stress this year.
How do you put up with such a difficult boss?

fillInExercises.sentence: I can't ___ why the app keeps crashing. It's really frustrating.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Figure out' means to find the answer to something by thinking.

fillInExercises.sentence: We need to ___ out a plan before the client meeting on Friday.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Work out' means to develop or figure out a plan.

fillInExercises.sentence: The technician will ___ the internet issue before the end of the day.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Sort out' means to solve a problem or deal with a situation.

fillInExercises.sentence: She ___ out a mistake in the contract that could have cost us money.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Point out' (past: pointed out) means to draw attention to something.

fillInExercises.sentence: We need someone to ___ a new marketing idea for the campaign.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Come up with' means to produce or think of an idea.

fillInExercises.sentence: I refuse to ___ up with rude behaviour from customers.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Put up with' means to tolerate something unpleasant.

fillInExercises.sentence: Everything ___ well in the end — the project was a success.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Work out' also means that something ended satisfactorily.

fillInExercises.sentence: Could you ___ out which option is cheaper? I'm confused.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Figure out' means to understand or calculate something.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: I can't [figure out] why the system keeps crashing.

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Let me have a look. I think I can [work out] what's causing it.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: I've been [put up with] this issue for a whole week.

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: Let me [point out] the error in the code — it's right here.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Who [come up with] this method? It's completely wrong.

lines.speaker: Hana
lines.text: I'll [sort out] the whole thing. Give me 20 minutes.

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.text: We need to [come up with] an idea for the presentation by Friday.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: I've been trying to [figure out] a good topic. How about AI?

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.text: Love it. Can we [work out] who does which part?

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Sure. But I want to [point out] that we only have three days.

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.text: I know. We'll have to [put up with] some late nights this week.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: It'll all [sort out] in the end if we stay organised.
